root_text.an5.144:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā sākete viharati tikaṇḍakīvane.
root_text.an5.144:2.1: “Sādhu, bhikkhave, bhikkhu kālena kālaṁ appaṭikūle paṭikūlasaññī vihareyya.
root_text.an5.144:2.2: Sādhu, bhikkhave, bhikkhu kālena kālaṁ paṭikūle appaṭikūlasaññī vihareyya.
root_text.an5.144:2.3: Sādhu, bhikkhave, bhikkhu kālena kālaṁ appaṭikūle ca paṭikūle ca paṭikūlasaññī vihareyya.
root_text.an5.144:2.4: Sādhu, bhikkhave, bhikkhu kālena kālaṁ paṭikūle ca appaṭikūle ca appaṭikūlasaññī vihareyya.
root_text.an5.144:2.5: Sādhu, bhikkhave, bhikkhu kālena kālaṁ paṭikūlañca appaṭikūlañca tadubhayaṁ abhinivajjetvā upekkhako vihareyya sato sampajāno.
root_text.an5.144:3.1: Kiñca, bhikkhave, bhikkhu atthavasaṁ paṭicca appaṭikūle paṭikūlasaññī vihareyya?
root_text.an5.144:3.3: idaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu atthavasaṁ paṭicca appaṭikūle paṭikūlasaññī vihareyya.
root_text.an5.144:4.1: Kiñca, bhikkhave, bhikkhu atthavasaṁ paṭicca paṭikūle appaṭikūlasaññī vihareyya?
root_text.an5.144:4.3: idaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu atthavasaṁ paṭicca paṭikūle appaṭikūlasaññī vihareyya.
root_text.an5.144:5.1: Kiñca, bhikkhave, bhikkhu atthavasaṁ paṭicca appaṭikūle ca paṭikūle ca paṭikūlasaññī vihareyya?
root_text.an5.144:5.2: ‘Mā me rajanīyesu dhammesu rāgo udapādi, mā me dosanīyesu dhammesu doso udapādī’ti—
root_text.an5.144:5.3: idaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu atthavasaṁ paṭicca appaṭikūle ca paṭikūle ca paṭikūlasaññī vihareyya.
root_text.an5.144:6.1: Kiñca, bhikkhave, bhikkhu atthavasaṁ paṭicca paṭikūle ca appaṭikūle ca appaṭikūlasaññī vihareyya?
root_text.an5.144:6.2: ‘Mā me dosanīyesu dhammesu doso udapādi, mā me rajanīyesu dhammesu rāgo udapādī’ti—
root_text.an5.144:6.3: idaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu atthavasaṁ paṭicca paṭikūle ca appaṭikūle ca appaṭikūlasaññī vihareyya.
root_text.an5.144:7.1: Kiñca, bhikkhave, bhikkhu atthavasaṁ paṭicca paṭikūlañca appaṭikūlañca tadubhayaṁ abhinivajjetvā upekkhako vihareyya?
root_text.an5.144:7.2: ‘Sato sampajāno mā me kvacani katthaci kiñcanaṁ rajanīyesu dhammesu rāgo udapādi, mā me kvacani katthaci kiñcanaṁ dosanīyesu dhammesu doso udapādi, mā me kvacani katthaci kiñcanaṁ mohanīyesu dhammesu moho udapādī’ti—
root_text.an5.144:7.3: idaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu atthavasaṁ paṭicca paṭikūlañca appaṭikūlañca tadubhayaṁ abhinivajjetvā upekkhako vihareyya sato sampajāno”ti.

translation_text.an5.144:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying near Sāketa, in Tikaṇḍakī Wood.
translation_text.an5.144:1.2: There the Buddha addressed the mendicants,
translation_text.an5.144:2.1: “Mendicants, a mendicant would do well to meditate from time to time perceiving the following: the repulsive in the unrepulsive,
translation_text.an5.144:2.3: the repulsive in both the unrepulsive and the repulsive, and
translation_text.an5.144:2.4: the unrepulsive in both the repulsive and the unrepulsive.
translation_text.an5.144:2.5: A mendicant would do well to meditate from time to time staying equanimous, mindful and aware, shunning both the repulsive and the unrepulsive.
translation_text.an5.144:3.1: For what reason should a mendicant meditate perceiving the repulsive in the unrepulsive?
translation_text.an5.144:3.2: ‘May greed not arise in me for things that arouse greed.’
translation_text.an5.144:3.3: A mendicant should meditate perceiving the repulsive in the unrepulsive for this reason.
translation_text.an5.144:4.1: For what reason should a mendicant meditate perceiving the unrepulsive in the repulsive?
translation_text.an5.144:4.2: ‘May hate not arise in me for things that provoke hate.’ …
translation_text.an5.144:5.1: For what reason should a mendicant meditate perceiving the repulsive in both the unrepulsive and the repulsive?
translation_text.an5.144:5.2: ‘May greed not arise in me for things that arouse greed. May hate not arise in me for things that provoke hate.’ …
translation_text.an5.144:6.1: For what reason should a mendicant meditate perceiving the unrepulsive in both the repulsive and the unrepulsive?
translation_text.an5.144:6.2: ‘May hate not arise in me for things that provoke hate. May greed not arise in me for things that arouse greed.’ …
translation_text.an5.144:7.1: For what reason should a mendicant meditate staying equanimous, mindful and aware, shunning both the repulsive and the unrepulsive?
translation_text.an5.144:7.2: ‘May no greed for things that arouse greed, hate for things that provoke hate, or delusion for things that promote delusion arise in me in any way at all.’
translation_text.an5.144:7.3: For this reason a mendicant should meditate staying equanimous, mindful and aware, shunning both the repulsive and the unrepulsive.”

variant_text.an5.144:2.1: appaṭikūle paṭikūlasaññī → appaṭikkūle paṭikkūlasaññī (bj, sya-all, km, pts1ed)
variant_text.an5.144:7.2: kvacani → kvacini (bj, sya-all, pts1ed) | kiñcanaṁ → kiñcana (bj, pts1ed); kiñcina (sya-all)
